jarvis.name: jarvis
jarvis.title: Jarvis
jarvis.subtitle: ai server
jarvis.description: Personal AI orchestrator running on a Mac Studio. Discord bot + judge loop + overnight Claude Code runs. Notes on the build.

posts.title: RAG over a personal vault
posts.summary: BGE embeddings, ChromaDB, and a privacy tag system that keeps personal notes off the network.

posts.title: Running it overnight
posts.summary: Queue, resource gates, crash recovery, and the 47-Discord-message disaster that taught me to coalesce notifications.

posts.title: The judge loop
posts.summary: A local Qwen 2.5 32B reads Claude Code's diffs and decides whether to accept them. The bugs that broke it twice.

posts.title: What Jarvis actually is
posts.summary: The architecture in one paragraph, then the parts that actually mattered. Five routing paths, six background workers.

posts.title: Why the Mac Studio
posts.summary: I almost returned the M3 Ultra. Here's what changed my mind about keeping a 96GB unified memory machine.

atlas.name: atlas
atlas.title: Atlas
atlas.subtitle: self-hosted chatgpt
atlas.description: A web app that puts my local models, my vault, and tool calls behind a single chat interface. Same machine as Jarvis, different shape.

posts.title: Tunneling and auth for a one-person app
posts.summary: Cloudflare Tunnel + Supabase magic links. Why this is overkill for one user and why I did it anyway.

posts.title: One large model at a time
posts.summary: 96GB sounds like a lot until you try loading two 70B models. The serializer that makes model switching honest.

posts.title: Hello, Atlas
posts.summary: Why a self-hosted ChatGPT made sense on top of an existing AI server, and why it's a separate codebase from Jarvis.
